posts.caption:
today.

come through, find your way around, and be part of it.

everything is already in motion.

maydayontheharbor.com
#MayDayOnTheHarbor

it's almost time.

we're getting everything ready across the harbor.

if you've been meaning to come, this is your reminder.

maydayontheharbor.com
#MayDayOnTheHarbor

expect a mix of things happening at once.

art installations, music, hands-on projects, workshops, and people moving between spaces.

you don't need a plan. just start somewhere and follow what pulls you in.

maydayontheharbor.com
#MayDayOnTheHarbor

free. all ages. open to everyone.

everything is happening inside the building, with different spaces set up for art, the scavenger hunt, and everything else going on throughout the day.

come for an hour or stay all day. move through it at your own pace.

masks are required and will be provided at the door.

full details are on the site.

maydayontheharbor.com
#MayDayOnTheHarbor

this isn't about a single day.

it's about practicing what it looks like to gather, create, and support each other outside of the usual systems.

may day is just the moment we choose to do it together.

maydayontheharbor.com
#MayDayOnTheHarbor

we take care of each other or we don't make it.

may day on the harbor is rooted in mutual aid, shared space, and showing up for each other in real ways.

this is about building something together, not just attending it.

maydayontheharbor.com
#MayDayOnTheHarbor

start somewhere.

you don't need a plan, a schedule, or a 'right way' to do this.

there will be things happening across the harbor all day. art being made, music, the scavenger hunt, people moving between spaces and building something together in real time.

wander. follow what pulls you. double back. stay where it feels right.

you're not late. you're not behind. just come through.

maydayontheharbor.com
#MayDayOnTheHarbor

there's a second version of the city hiding in plain sight.

the scavenger hunt moves through the harbor, buildings, and in-between spaces most people walk past.

you'll solve riddles, find locations, and uncover pieces as you go.

play solo or with friends. start point is on the site.

maydayontheharbor.com
#MayDayOnTheHarbor

this is where things get made.

inside the art center we're setting up open tables for zines, prints, collaborative pieces, and whatever people bring with them.

you can sit down, try something new, or just make a mess and see what comes out of it.

no experience needed. no pressure to be good at anything.

maydayontheharbor.com
#MayDayOnTheHarbor

art is not extra. it's how we remember and how we speak when everything else falls short.

we're opening a call for work as part of may day on the harbor. bring something you've made, something in progress, or something you want to share with the community.

no gatekeeping. no 'right way' to do it.

submission info is on the site.

maydayontheharbor.com
#MayDayOnTheHarbor

this isn't a festival you pass through.

it's something we build together for a day and carry forward after.

may day on the harbor brings people into the same space to make things, share skills, and meet each other outside of the usual roles.

come as you are. stay as long as you want.

maydayontheharbor.com
#MayDayOnTheHarbor

may day on the harbor is coming back.

a day built by workers, artists, and neighbors. not a stage to watch from a distance, but something you step into.

we're taking over spaces across the harbor with art, music, workshops, and projects you can actually be part of.

details, schedule, and how to get involved are on the site.

maydayontheharbor.com
#MayDayOnTheHarbor
